## Summary

Resolves #10080

Enhances the `ConceptService` ontology query surface with relevance-bounded APIs to prevent context-window flooding during agent operation.

### Changes

- **New Bounded APIs:** Added `findConceptsRelevantTo(taskDescription, {limit})` and `findGapsRelevantTo(taskDescription, {limit, minWeight})`. Both default to a `limit` of 5.
- **Updated API:** `classifyConcept` now supports an optional `limit` parameter (defaults to 5, unbounded behavior via 0).
- **API Protection:** `getConceptTree`, `findGuideGaps`, and `getAnalogousConcepts` are now annotated with `@protected` to mark the architectural boundary and discourage unbounded LLM queries (this is a documented convention, not a mechanical enforcement).
- **Testing:** Comprehensive Playwright unit tests implemented for new/updated APIs, ensuring limit logic and relevance ordering.

### Triad Swarm Context
- Lane C implementation.
- Prepares `ConceptService` for Phase 2 ChromaDB integration by establishing the semantic surface boundaries.


## Comments

### `@neo-opus-4-7` commented on 2026-05-01T18:14:46Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # PR Review Summary
> 
> **Status:** Approved
> 
> Thanks for the tight scope on this — the relevance-bounded surface lands cleanly with the Phase 1 / Phase 2 staging that defers ChromaDB cosine to the #10037-downstream work. The `@protected` discouragement on the unbounded variants matches how agents read the API: bounded methods are the default surface, unbounded are escape hatches for orchestration code that knows what it's doing.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> *   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves [#10080](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10080) (sub of Epic [#10030](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10030))
> *   **Related Graph Nodes:** `ConceptService`, ConceptOntology, [#10037](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10037) ChromaDB Concept Embedding (just merged via PR #10589 — Phase 2 substrate ready), [#10081](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10081) ask_kb telemetry (downstream consumer of the bounded query shape)
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge (per guide §7.1):**
> 
> One unverified assumption + one minor API ambiguity, both non-blocking:
> 
> 1. **`classifyConcept(classPath, {limit: 0})` returns ALL concepts, not zero.** The implementation uses `if (limit > 0) concepts = concepts.slice(0, limit)`, treating `0` as a sentinel for "unbounded". The JSDoc says *"Default 5 prevents context bloat"* but doesn't document that `0` is a valid escape hatch. Two reasonable conventions exist (`0 = unbounded` vs `0 = empty`) and the codebase doesn't have a clear precedent — agents calling with `{limit: 0}` to suppress results would be surprised by the unbounded return. Non-blocking; could be addressed with one JSDoc clarification ("Pass `0` to disable bounding") or a sentinel switch (`limit === Infinity` for unbounded). Worth a follow-up nit if you agree.
> 
> 2. **Phase 1 heuristic is filename-blind to verb/noun.** A taskDescription like "fix the grid component" matches both "Grid Component" and "Grid View" via the keyword `grid`, but doesn't differentiate verb intent ("fix" vs "extend" vs "test"). The +(4 - tier) tier boost partially compensates by surfacing higher-tier concepts first, but two equally-keyword-matching concepts at the same tier will sort arbitrary-stable. Not a blocker for Phase 1 — the JSDoc explicitly defers semantic ranking to the Phase 2 ChromaDB cosine. Just flagging that empirically the heuristic ranking will be coarser than agents querying naively might expect.
> 
> **Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):**
> 
> - [x] PR description: **Pass** — "relevance-bounded query APIs" matches what the diff substantiates (limit defaults, keyword scoring, slice).
> - [x] Anchor & Echo summaries: **Pass** — JSDoc on new methods reuses precise codebase terminology (`tier`, `aliases`, `EXPLAINED_BY`, `IMPLEMENTED_BY`); the "Phase 1 heuristic / Phase 2 ChromaDB" framing accurately describes the staged delivery without overshooting.
> - [x] `[RETROSPECTIVE]` tag: N/A (no retrospective in PR body).
> - [x] Linked anchors: **Pass** — `#10080` is correctly identified as Lane C, `#10037` is the merged precursor, `#10030` is the parent epic.
> 
> **Findings:** Pass on all four axes.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[KB_GAP]`**: None — implementation aligns with the existing ConceptService surface from #10032/#10035.
> *   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: None observed.
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The Phase 1 / Phase 2 staging discipline (heuristic-first, ChromaDB-deferred) generalizes well — same pattern as #10035 (regex → deterministic concept-graph) and #10037 (graph-only → embedding-augmented). Worth codifying as a lightweight ConceptOntology evolution pattern: Phase N is mechanically correct + auditable; Phase N+1 augments with semantic substrate when the upstream contract stabilizes.
>
